summary: Penrose measures its own detector before any verdict is trusted: specificity (does it reject noise?) and sensitivity (can it find a real edge, and how strong must it be?), on real BTC returns.

controls.name: Placebo · negative control
controls.detail: No-edge AR(1) signals on real returns at zero cost; DSR-over-noise p95 = 0.91 (below the 0.95 bar). 4/100 reached soft 'watch'. A pass can only come from the statistical gates rejecting noise.

controls.name: 5-null battery
controls.detail: white-noise · regime-vol · bid-ask-bounce · zero-alpha · GARCH — each strips signal but keeps a data mechanic naive backtests mistake for edge. None certified.

controls.name: Injection · positive control
controls.detail: IC=0 certifies 0% (specificity holds); the certify path is reachable under measured costs. The output is a detection curve, not a pass/fail.

controls.name: Native breadth
controls.detail: IR = IC·√breadth. The certify-IC floor falls ~1/√N cross-sectionally, pulling realistic 0.03–0.05 edges from 'underpowered' into testable — why 'underpowered' is a power artifact, not a blind spot.

controls.name: Sensitivity sweep
controls.detail: The single-asset detection floor as a function of the power levers: it falls with sample size and rises with cost — a defensible curve, not a single number.

caveat: Preliminary engineering controls, not yet field-level calibrated error rates. The null battery is finite and partly synthetic; the injection/breadth thresholds use a specific injected data-generating process; some results are cost-assumption dependent. Reruns from a frozen artifact are on the roadmap before publication.
